title: LLM Logbook Schema
description: Schema for tracking development context across sessions. Supports long-term memory, objective tracking with actionable guidance, and progress documentation. Designed for continuity across multiple LLMs and sessions.

ticket.description: Metadata of the ticket or task that anchors this logbook. This is the ultimate goal that all objectives contribute to.
ticket.$comment: Populate from user input or external system. If no ticket URL exists, leave url empty. Title and description are required to create the logbook.

title.description: Title of the ticket or task. Should be concise and descriptive.
title.$comment: Copy from ticket system or ask user. Keep under 100 characters.

url.description: Direct URL to the ticket or task in an external system (Jira, GitHub, etc.).
url.$comment: Optional. Leave empty string if no external system. Do not invent URLs.

description.description: Detailed explanation and context provided in the ticket. Includes acceptance criteria if available.
description.$comment: Copy from ticket or ask user to explain. Include all relevant details: requirements, constraints, acceptance criteria.

objectives.description: Project objectives organized by priority. Main objectives define high-level deliverables with scope. Secondary objectives are actionable steps with specific completion guidance.
objectives.$comment: Both main and secondary arrays can be populated in the same operation. Secondary objectives should have completion_guide based on code analysis using main objectives' scope.files as reference points.

main.description: Primary objectives that define major deliverables. Each provides context, reference files, and applicable rules.
main.$comment: Use project_manifest.json to identify related layers and features. Each main objective needs: verifiable content, business context, scope with reference files, and applicable rules from project_rules.json.

secondary.description: Actionable objectives derived from analysis of main objectives. Each contains specific guidance for implementation.
secondary.$comment: Base completion_guide on code inspection starting from main objectives' scope.files. Apply scope.rules when writing guidance. All secondary objectives contribute to ticket completion.

recent_context.description: Running log of recent interactions, findings, decisions, and progress. Newest entries at index 0. Sliding window that compacts to history_summary when full.
recent_context.$comment: Prepend new entries at index 0. When array exceeds maxItems, remove last (oldest) entry and summarize it to history_summary. Never replace entire array.

items.description: Single context entry representing a meaningful interaction, finding, or decision.
items.$comment: Create entry for: progress updates, important findings, decisions made, blockers encountered, user instructions. Keep content focused and actionable.

id.description: Unique identifier for this entry. Immutable once created.
id.$comment: Start at 1, increment by 1 globally across all entries. Never reuse IDs.

created_at.description: UTC timestamp when entry was created (ISO 8601). Immutable.
created_at.$comment: Set once at creation. Never modify.

content.description: Entry content describing action, decision, finding, or progress. Should be meaningful and provide context for future sessions.
content.$comment: Write concisely but completely. Include: what happened, why it matters, any decisions made. Future agents will use this to understand context.

mood.description: User's emotional state during this entry if observable (focused, frustrated, excited, uncertain).
mood.$comment: Optional. Only populate if user mood is clearly observable. Helps future agents adjust communication style.

history_summary.description: Compact summaries of entries removed from recent_context. Preserves continuity when short-term memory is full. Newest at index 0.
history_summary.$comment: Write here ONLY when truncating recent_context. Summarize removed entry in max 140 chars. Prepend at index 0. If exceeds maxItems, remove oldest (last) entry.

items.description: Compressed summary of a removed recent_context entry.
items.$comment: Capture essential information only: what was done, key decision, or important finding.

id.description: Unique identifier for this summary. Immutable once created.
id.$comment: Start at 1, increment by 1. Separate sequence from recent_context IDs.

created_at.description: UTC timestamp when summary was created (ISO 8601). Immutable.
created_at.$comment: Set once at creation. Never modify.

content.description: Compressed summary of removed entry. Must capture essential information.
content.$comment: Summarize in tweet-length. Focus on: what was done, outcome, or key decision. Omit details recoverable from code.

mood.description: User's emotional state at time of original entry if it was recorded.
mood.$comment: Optional. Preserve from original entry if present.

future_reminders.description: User-created reminders for future sessions. Agent surfaces these when their time arrives.
future_reminders.$comment: Only add reminders when user explicitly requests. Check this array at session start and surface any due reminders.

items.description: A scheduled reminder to surface in a future session.
items.$comment: Create only on explicit user request. Remove after surfacing to user.

id.description: Unique identifier for this reminder. Immutable once created.
id.$comment: Start at 1, increment by 1. Never reuse IDs.

created_at.description: UTC timestamp when reminder was created (ISO 8601). Immutable.
created_at.$comment: Set once at creation. Never modify.

content.description: Reminder message to surface to user when due.
content.$comment: Write exactly what user wants to be reminded about. Include any context needed.

when.description: UTC timestamp when this reminder should be surfaced to user.
when.$comment: Compare against current time at session start. Surface if current time >= when.

recommendations.description: Recommendations discovered during implementation. These are improvements or follow-ups that aren't blocking but should be considered.
recommendations.$comment: Populated by objectives-implement command when code-implementer finds opportunities for improvement. Review periodically.

created_at.description: UTC timestamp when recommendation was created.

from_objective.description: ID of the objective that generated this recommendation (e.g., '1.1').

affected_files.description: Files that would be affected by implementing this recommendation.

priority.description: Priority level for addressing this recommendation.

main_objective.description: High-level objective defining a major deliverable. Provides business context, reference files as starting points for code tracing, and applicable project rules.
main_objective.$comment: Use project_manifest.json to identify related layers and features. Populate scope.files with entry points for code analysis. Populate scope.rules with applicable rule IDs from project_rules.json (empty array if no rules exist).

id.description: Unique identifier for this main objective. Immutable once created.
id.$comment: Start at 1, increment by 1. Never reuse IDs even after abandonment.

created_at.description: UTC timestamp when objective was created (ISO 8601). Immutable.
created_at.$comment: Set once at creation. Never modify.

content.description: Verifiable outcome statement that serves as acceptance criteria. When this statement is true, the objective is achieved.
content.$comment: Write as outcome, not task. Validate: Can you answer 'Is this done?' with yes/no by inspecting code? If not, rewrite.

context.description: Business or technical rationale: who needs this, what problem it solves, relevant constraints or dependencies.
context.$comment: Include information that helps make implementation decisions. Reference stakeholders, dependencies, or deadlines if relevant.

scope.description: Implementation boundaries: starting point files for code tracing and project rules that constrain the solution.
scope.$comment: Files are entry points for deeper analysis, not exhaustive lists. Rules guide implementation approach.

files.description: Reference files identified during initial analysis. Entry points for deeper code tracing.
files.$comment: Use relative paths from project root. Append '(new)' for files to create. Example: 'src/dtos/ProductDTO.ts (new)'.

rules.description: IDs from project_rules.json that apply to this objective's scope. Empty array if project has no rules defined.
rules.$comment: Reference rules by ID. Apply these rules when implementing. If no rules exist, use the most common best practices for the framework and language always applying YAGNI principle.

status.description: Current state: not_started (pending work), active (in progress), blocked (waiting on external input), achieved (completed), abandoned (cancelled).
status.$comment: Set only ONE main objective as 'active' at a time. Use 'blocked' when waiting for user input or external dependency. Set 'achieved' when content statement is verified true.

secondary_objective.description: Actionable objective derived from code analysis. Contains specific implementation guidance based on actual code inspection.
secondary_objective.$comment: Base completion_guide on code inspection using main objectives' scope.files as starting points. Reference specific files, patterns, and applicable rules in the guide.

id.description: Unique identifier across all secondary objectives. Immutable once created.
id.$comment: Start at 1, increment globally across all secondary objectives. Never reuse IDs.

created_at.description: UTC timestamp when objective was created (ISO 8601). Immutable.
created_at.$comment: Set once at creation. Never modify.

content.description: Specific verifiable outcome that contributes to ticket completion. Should be completable in a focused work session.
content.$comment: Be granular. Validate: Can this be completed and verified in one session? If not, split into multiple objectives.

completion_guide.description: Step-by-step implementation guidance based on code analysis. References specific files, patterns, line numbers, and applicable rules + YAGNI principle applied to the design.
completion_guide.$comment: Each item must reference actual code or rules. Format: 'Use pattern from src/file.ts:45' or 'Apply rule #7: description'. No generic advice. When creating each item always apply the YAGNI principle.

status.description: Current state: not_started (pending work), active (in progress), blocked (waiting on external input), achieved (completed), abandoned (cancelled).
status.$comment: Set only ONE secondary objective as 'active' at a time. Work sequentially. Set 'achieved' after verifying content statement is true in codebase.
